narrative: As an Asian male applicant, your law school application was evaluated using a predictive model that assesses the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try, based on academic and background factors from past students. The model estimated a 61% probability that you would fail the bar exam, which crossed the threshold for rejection, as schools prioritize admitting those predicted to succeed. This decision hinged on just a few key aspects of your profile, with your Year 3 school decile ranking emerging as the strongest influence pushing toward a failure prediction—your position in the 3rd decile (below the typical 5.5th) signaled weaker performance relative to peers at that stage. Close behind, your LSAT score of 33, lower than the average of about 37, also pulled the prediction toward failure, as higher scores are strongly linked to bar success in the model's view. On a more positive note, your strong undergraduate GPA of 3.90—well above the typical 3.21—worked significantly in your favor, countering some of the risk by demonstrating solid academic capability earlier on. Your middle family income bracket and full-time student status aligned with the norm for most applicants (over 90% are full-time), so they played neutral roles here. Compared to typical students who pass, your later-year grades and LSAT stood out as below average, overshadowing your impressive undergrad record despite your decile1 of 4th (still under the usual 5.7th). This combination made the model cautious, viewing you as higher risk than the average successful candidate. While disappointing, this isn't a judgment on your potential as an Asian male with clear strengths—many in similar positions improve with targeted effort. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score toward or above 37, and if possible, strengthen your recent academic decile through additional coursework or post-bac programs that highlight upward trends. Building a narrative around your high UGPA in appeals or reapplications could also help, perhaps with letters emphasizing your resilience. Finally, exploring schools with holistic reviews or bar passage support programs might better align with your profile. Keep pushing—you've already shown you can excel academically.
